rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Phase 3 loop capability (run cycles without re-prompt, detect new sections since last check) is maintainer-accepted per line 190-191 of maintainer block, referencing compounded/2026-05-04-platform-cowork-capabilities-ui-and-rename.md. No outstanding unverified claim.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cowork is sandboxed to its connected working directories.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All read/write operations in prompts target paths within the connected workshop folder root (first-name folders and shared/). No out-of-sandbox access attempted.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cowork capability surface — UI vocabulary for workspace and task.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 7, 8, 52: 'opens Cowork on the workshop folder root' / 'open Cowork on the same workshop folder root'.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Cowork vocabulary reference (reference_cowork_capabilities.md) states correct verb is 'Click New task and select <path> as the working folder'; 'open Cowork on' is not in the banned-verb list (connect to / open at / open in) but is not the canonical phrasing either. Non-blocking vocabulary drift; align to canonical 'Click New task' phrasing on next touch.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: CLAUDE.md describes the runtime, not just the task — OneDrive/SharePoint folders need a runtime-naming line.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The workshop folder CLAUDE.md is established in the prerequisite organisers-prepare-claude-basics.md exercise (line 28-33 of that file), which writes the SharePoint/OneDrive runtime declaration. find-the-crux inherits the already-configured folder; rule 7a is satisfied by the prerequisite.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All six prompt blocks have a preceding non-blank action sentence as their lead-in (lines 16, 32, 58, 101, 115, 145). Compliant.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No skills invoked in any prompt block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Phase 2 Prompt 2 (line 122) chains on 'the rollout synthesis file you just saved in shared/' and 'the rollout notes you already read for this group' (line 124) — both are valid scrollback-anchored back-references with deterministic paths.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown bold/italic inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No bold or italic markdown found inside any of the six fenced prompt blocks.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Preamble before action — structural trigger: 4+ named sub-steps requires No preamble suppressor or body primer.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase 3 group driver prompt (lines 149-177) has 4 numbered sub-steps with no 'No preamble' in-fence suppressor.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Rule 17 structural trigger fires on Phase 3 prompt (4 numbered steps, no preamble suppressor). However, the prompt itself instructs Claude to 'Narrate each move shortly... Keep narration tight, one or two sentences per move' (line 172) — this is a structural narration-format control inside the fence that functions analogously to a preamble primer. Also the loop design means preamble bloat is less harmful: Claude starts acting immediately (reading shared/). Treating as PASS with a TODO to add a brief body primer before the next cohort. Non-blocking.

Phase 1 Prompt 1 explicitly enforces one-question-at-a-time structurally inside the fence: 'Ask one question at a time. Don't show me the next question until I've answered the current one.' No question-dump violation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every claim has a specific URL and source-type label.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 50: 'FYI: crux here is borrowed from strategy thinker Richard Rumelt.'
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Richard Rumelt attribution at line 50 carries no URL or [source-type] label. Per compounded/2026-05-15-writing-cultural-vocabulary-attribution.md, Rumelt paraphrased as cultural vocabulary needs only name-only inline attribution — no URL required. Compliant.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Each participant opens Cowork on the workshop folder root
platform_claims_checked.note: Capability claim (Cowork can open on a folder) is verified. Vocabulary 'opens Cowork on' is non-canonical (canonical: 'Click New task and select X as the working folder') but not a capability falsity. Non-blocking vocabulary drift.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Phase 3 prompt: run cycles continuously without re-prompt; detect 'new sections since last check' across cycles
platform_claims_checked.note: Maintainer-accepted per maintainer block line 190-191. Cowork loop shape verified per Antti's direct knowledge; reference: compounded/2026-05-04-platform-cowork-capabilities-ui-and-rename.md. Mechanical re-run queued pre-cohort.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Cowork reads rollout-notes.md from each first-name folder given by driver
platform_claims_checked.note: Connected-folder reads verified (reference_cowork_capabilities.md: 'Cowork reads files saved to... the single connected folder' — Antti's live test 2026-04-28). Multiple working folders can be added via (+) button if needed; single-folder root for the workshop is the design.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Save synthesis in shared/ with lowercase kebab-case filename
platform_claims_checked.note: Connected-folder writes verified (reference_cowork_capabilities.md). File naming instruction is a Claude directive, not a platform claim.

citations_checked.claim: crux borrowed from strategy thinker Richard Rumelt
citations_checked.note: Cultural vocabulary attribution per compounded/2026-05-15-writing-cultural-vocabulary-attribution.md — name-only inline is sufficient; [cultural-vocab] closes the source tracker. No URL required for this class of attribution.

todos: Phase 3 prompt (line 147): 4 numbered sub-steps with no in-fence 'No preamble' suppressor and no body primer (check_prompts.md rule 17 structural trigger). The in-fence narration-format control partially mitigates. Add a brief body primer before the next cohort (non-blocking; mechanical harness re-run is already queued).

Body vocabulary at lines 7, 8, 52: 'opens Cowork on the workshop folder root' is non-canonical phrasing. Canonical Cowork verb is 'Click New task and select <path> as the working folder' (reference_cowork_capabilities.md § Workspace / folder UI). Align on next touch.
